deep_dive.title: Measuring What Matters: A Unified Evaluation Framework for GNN Explainability
deep_dive.abstract: Graph eXplainable AI (G-XAI) is increasingly important for making Graph Neural Networks interpretable and accountable. While a growing number of explainers are available, choosing the right method and assessing the trustworthiness of its outputs remains unclear. Consistent evaluation practices and actionable guidance are still missing, hindering practical adoption. In this paper, we introduce a unified, quantitative benchmarking framework for G-XAI that requires no ground-truth assumptions. We formalize tabular explainability metrics for graph data, evaluating topological structure and node features as independent components. Our large-scale benchmarking study identifies explainers that consistently lie on the Pareto front across metric pairs and tasks, establishing robustly non-dominated solutions - while confirming that no single explainer achieves universal superiority. We distill our findings into actionable G-XAI usability guidelines to support Machine Learning practitioners in evaluating and deploying trustworthy GNN-based pipelines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: GNN explainability evaluation framework for graph-based brain disorder diagnosis
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 图神经网络可解释性评估框架，可用于脑疾病诊断
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 图神经网络可解释性评估框架，可用于脑疾病诊断

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a unified evaluation framework for GNN explainability, benchmarking explainers on graph data without ground truth, relevant for trustworthy brain network analysis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有图神经网络可解释性评估缺乏统一标准。本文提出一个无需真值的量化基准框架，将表格可解释性指标适配到图数据，独立评估拓扑结构和节点特征。大规模实验发现多个解释器在指标对上的帕累托前沿。该框架可直接应用于基于图神经网络的脑网络分析，提升模型可解释性评估的一致性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有图神经网络可解释性评估缺乏统一标准。本文提出一个无需真值的量化基准框架，将表格可解释性指标适配到图数据，独立评估拓扑结构和节点特征。大规模实验发现多个解释器在指标对上的帕累托前沿。该框架可直接应用于基于图神经网络的脑网络分析，提升模型可解释性评估的一致性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Find research papers using graph neural networks or deep learning for resting state fMRI functional connectivity and brain disorder diagnosis.

quick_skim.title: Lightweight ML-Based Automatic Sleep Staging Framework with Constrained CNN and Mamba for Small-Sample EEG Datasets
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic sleep staging is a key technology for precise diagnosis and treatment of sleep disorders as well as long-term home sleep monitoring. Portable electroencephalogram (EEG) devices have become the focus of research due to their convenience in data collection. However, current methods still face three major challenges: large parameter sizes that easily lead to overfitting on small datasets, low accuracy in classifying difficult stages such as N1 and REM, unclear optimal training dataset size, and difficulty in deployment. This paper proposes GamSleepNet, a lightweight and low-latency automatic sleep staging framework for single-channel EEG. The framework features the FEB module, which combines improved Gabor kernels with learnable filters for feature extraction, uses the Mamba architecture to build a temporal classification network, introduces a novel contrastive loss and a two-stage training strategy, and experimentally validates the optimal dataset size for single-channel EEG sleep staging models. On the Sleepedf dataset, this model achieves an overall accuracy of 87.86 percent with only 30.86 thousand parameters, with all metrics reaching SOTA levels and significantly improving the identification accuracy of challenging sleep stages.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: lightweight ML for automatic sleep staging from EEG, neuroimaging AI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 轻量级机器学习用于EEG自动睡眠分期，神经影像AI
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 轻量级机器学习用于EEG自动睡眠分期，神经影像AI
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 轻量级机器学习用于EEG自动睡眠分期，神经影像AI
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GamSleepNet, a lightweight CNN-Mamba framework for automatic sleep staging from single-channel EEG, addressing small-sample overfitting and difficult stages.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 便携式EEG设备在家庭睡眠监测中前景广阔，但现有模型参数量大易过拟合，且对N1和REM等困难分期准确率低。本文提出GamSleepNet轻量框架，包括FEB模块和改进的Mamba模块，在单通道EEG上实现低延迟自动睡眠分期。在小样本数据集上优于现有方法，且易于部署。

quick_skim.title: Graph-Regularized Deep Learning for EEG-Based Emotion Recognition with Psychologically-Grounded Label Structure
quick_skim.abstract: EEG-based emotion recognition is critical for mental health monitoring and affective brain-computer interfaces, yet existing deep learning approaches often treat emotion classes as isolated labels, ignoring their psychological interdependencies. We propose a graph-regularized learning framework that conceptualizes emotions as nodes in a graph where edges encode proximity based on dimensional emotion theories. We adapt three complementary regularization strategies--Graph Label Smoothing (intuitive soft labeling), Commuting distance on graph via Graph Laplacian (spectral graph theory), and Sliced Wasserstein Distance (optimal transport on graph)--ordered by increasing computational complexity. These strategies penalize model predictions that deviate from the established emotion topology. Our framework is evaluated across three representative backbone architectures: AudioTransformer (pure transformer), Conformer (CNN-transformer hybrid), and DCGNN (causal graph neural network), demonstrating architecture-agnostic benefits. Experiments on SEED-IV (4 classes) and SEED-V (5 classes) datasets show consistent improvements: best case up to +5.42% accuracy and 39% reduction in psychologically implausible misclassifications. Ultimately, our framework help raise the upper bound of performance achievable with standard approaches. Code will be released.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: graph-regularized deep learning for EEG emotion recognition, transferable to fMRI brain network analysis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 图正则化深度学习用于EEG情感识别，可迁移至fMRI脑网络分析

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes graph-regularized deep learning for EEG emotion recognition using psychologically-grounded label structure, relevant for graph-based brain network analysis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 基于EEG的情绪识别中，现有方法忽视情绪标签的心理依赖关系。本文提出图正则化框架，将情绪视为图中节点，边编码维度情绪理论的接近性。采用三种正则化策略：图标签平滑、图拉普拉斯距离和切片Wasserstein距离。在EEG数据集上验证了有效性，该方法可推广到其他图结构脑信号分析。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 基于EEG的情绪识别中，现有方法忽视情绪标签的心理依赖关系。本文提出图正则化框架，将情绪视为图中节点，边编码维度情绪理论的接近性。采用三种正则化策略：图标签平滑、图拉普拉斯距离和切片Wasserstein距离。在EEG数据集上验证了有效性，该方法可推广到其他图结构脑信号分析。

quick_skim.title: Progression as Latent Drift: Generative Forecasting of Slow-Evolving Pathologies
quick_skim.abstract: Forecasting the future anatomy of slow-evolving neurodegenerative diseases could enable earlier, more targeted intervention and improve clinical trial design, but it remains challenging because true progression signals are subtle in longitudinal MRI. In this low-signal regime, transferring modern generative sequence models directly is unreliable: training is dominated by stable baseline anatomy and confounded by dense, sample-specific nuisance variation. We first provide a theoretical analysis that explains these failures through two modes. Identity collapse occurs when optimization is driven toward reproducing the current anatomy, which prevents the model from learning faint temporal change. The continuous interpolation trap arises when standard smooth networks cannot separate localized biological drift from pervasive noise, which leads to spurious changes that diffuse across the volume. To address both issues, we propose Latent Drift, a progressive generative framework that learns change in a compressed semantic representation rather than synthesizing full-resolution anatomy. This design removes pixel-level identity from the prediction target and concentrates model capacity on progression-relevant dynamics. We further apply Finite Scalar Quantization to the learned change representation, which suppresses small, high-frequency nuisance fluctuations while preserving consistent structural drift. Experiments on longitudinal 3D brain MRI show that Latent Drift improves patient-specific neuro-forecasting over diffusion and autoregressive transformer baselines across generative fidelity and clinically relevant evaluation metrics. Project page: \href{https://cutepkq.github.io/latent-drift}{https://cutepkq.github.io/latent-drift}.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: generative forecasting of neurodegenerative disease progression using longitudinal MRI, neuroimaging AI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用纵向MRI生成预测神经退行性疾病进展，神经影像AI
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用纵向MRI生成预测神经退行性疾病进展，神经影像AI

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a generative model for forecasting slow-evolving neurodegenerative pathologies from longitudinal MRI, addressing identity collapse and interpolation trap.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对纵向MRI中微小进展信号难以学习的问题，提出一个生成式预测模型，通过理论分析揭示身份崩溃和连续插值陷阱，并设计方法避免这些失败模式，实现对神经退行性疾病的准确预测。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对纵向MRI中微小进展信号难以学习的问题，提出一个生成式预测模型，通过理论分析揭示身份崩溃和连续插值陷阱，并设计方法避免这些失败模式，实现对神经退行性疾病的准确预测。
